summary: The gate's corpus becomes committed artifacts plus the lock — retire temper.toml-as-corpus as the gate's read source

edit.description: `check` today gates against the committed `temper.toml` manifest as its corpus (main.rs ~700, keyed by bare kind, with the legacy live-extraction fallback). Re-point the gate: the engine consumes committed artifacts plus the lock's declaration rows (20-surface, 'Checking never needs a language runtime … consume committed artifacts plus the lock'; 50-distribution, 'The stranger gate'). No manifest read on the gate path.

The gate's surface load moves from manifest/`.temper` extraction to reading the lock's declaration rows + the committed `.claude/**` artifacts. Keep the diagnostic shape identical so reporters are untouched.
Freshness (disk vs lock) is the same one comparison in one vocabulary; wire the gate's corpus read through the lock rows this depends on.

tests.asserts: reconciled: the gate reads the lock's declaration rows + committed artifacts, not the manifest as corpus

acceptance: `temper check` gates a harness from committed artifacts plus the lock alone (no toolchain, no manifest-as-corpus); the embedded default program still gates an SDK-less harness (50-distribution, the stranger gate).
notes: Retires manifest-as-corpus on the GATE path only; the manifest machinery itself (roster/bindings artifacts, the reader) retires at MANIFEST-MACHINERY-RETIRE. Existing manifest-corpus tests reconcile to the lock-rows source.

summary: The SDK re-cuts to the six-noun face — harness(), kind<T>(), clause values, needs, file()/text/blocks, one JSON pipe to the engine

edit.description: Re-cut the authoring face to the six-noun model: `harness()` (members · expect · require · settings · reachability, 40-composition); `kind<T>()`/`genre<T>()` constructors with the five runtime facts (15-kinds); clauses as values; `needs` (permissions derived at emit from their union, 20-surface); the three Prose constructors `file()`/`` text`…` ``/`blocks()`. Every type erases at the seam — the SDK implements no semantics, piping one internal versioned JSON of declaration rows to the engine (20-surface, 'The seam — one implementation'). Emit stays total, byte-reproducible, double-emit verified.

Members become typed values with flat top-level typed fields (no `fields:` bag), `satisfies`/`requires`/`needs`, prose as a typed field (20-surface, 'The member'). Kind identity travels by import, never by string (15-kinds).

The SDK no longer authors a `temper.toml`/roster/bindings dialect (40-composition Decision rejects it); its output is the compiled declaration rows on the JSON pipe + the lock's declaration rows. Retire the manifest-authoring shape here, or fold it into the lock/seam producer.
`harness()` compiles the assembly to declaration rows; `expect` binding is the spread of an exported clause array (a floor), no binding table (40-composition, 'Binding is implicit').

Projection writes the `.claude/**` artifacts byte-faithfully; the permission list is the derived union of members' `needs`; hook/MCP members fold many-to-one into settings/`.mcp.json` (20-surface, 'Emit — total').
The lock stamps provenance + emit fingerprints + the NEW declaration rows the engine reads (matching LOCK-DECLARATION-ROWS' row shape — the byte-parity lockstep, kept in sync).

tests.asserts: harness()/kind<T>()/clause values/needs/file()/text/blocks compile; emit produces the declaration-row seam + byte-faithful projection + lock; double-emit byte-stable (validated by the afterMerge `sdk test` gate)

acceptance: The SDK authors a harness in the six-noun face and emits declaration rows (the engine's seam) + a byte-faithful projection + the lock; the `sdk test` gate is green.
notes: Cross-language (sdk/*.ts), validated by the afterMerge `sdk test` gate ((sdk-build-gate) RESOLVED). Serialized in-chain (not parallel) because it defines the seam the engine reads and the posture/needs model later links carry. Two byte-parity writers stay in lockstep until single-writer lands (human-gated).

summary: The CLI collapses to init·check·emit·install·bundle·schema — retire import/diff/session-start/why/requirements/impact/context; temper guard lands

edit.description: Collapse the `Command` enum to the corpus surface (20-surface, 'CLI surface'): keep init·check·emit·install·bundle·schema; RETIRE the `Import`, `Diff`, `SessionStart` (a verb — session start is a check reporter, not a verb, 50-distribution), `Why`, `Requirements`, `Impact`, `Context` variants and their dispatch arms. ADD `Command::Guard` — the `temper guard` subcommand reading the `PreToolUse` stdin payload, blocking per the author's declared surface-authority posture (note/warn/block), default advisory (50-distribution, the guard Decision; (guard-posture) resolved). `explain` is NOT added here — it lands fork-gated at EXPLAIN-UNIFY.

Replace the migration-era always-exit-0 guard grep (`install::guard_command`, ~454) with wiring for the `temper guard` subcommand at the provider write boundary; the enforcement posture is author-declared, install-wired, enumerated, self-audited (20-surface, 'surface authority is a declared posture').

The four read verbs' dispatch (why/impact/context/requirements) is removed from the CLI here; the read TRAVERSALS are re-homed under the single `explain` verb at EXPLAIN-UNIFY. Trim only what the retired CLI arms reference; leave the graph-walk helpers explain reuses.
Session-start is a check reporter, not a verb — reconcile these to drive `check` with the session-start reporter, not a `SessionStart` command.
The four-verb CLI tests reconcile: the verbs retire; their traversals move to explain (EXPLAIN-UNIFY). Trim/retarget to the surviving reporter/guard behavior.
Reconcile the CLI surface assertions to init·check·emit·install·bundle·schema·guard.

tests.asserts: the CLI exposes init·check·emit·install·bundle·schema·guard; the retired verbs are gone

acceptance: `temper --help` lists init·check·emit·install·bundle·schema·guard; `temper guard` reads a PreToolUse payload and blocks/warns/notes per the declared posture (default advisory); session-start is a check reporter.
notes: explain deferred to EXPLAIN-UNIFY (fork-gated on (explain-target-disambiguation)) so the demolition does not stall on an unresolved design question. Guard rides here per John's step-4 ('temper guard lands'); the posture model is carried by SDK-RECUT upstream.

summary: Retire the manifest machinery — temper.toml/roster.toml/bindings.toml + their reader and the contract/ byte-parity corpus

per.section: Decision: one authored assembly, no configuration dialect

edit.description: Remove the remaining manifest/roster/bindings load + emit-manifest paths now that the gate reads the lock rows (GATE-CORPUS-ARTIFACTS-LOCK) and the SDK authors no dialect (SDK-RECUT). 40-composition Decision rejects `temper.toml` and its `roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` decomposition — a second authoring surface.
Retire the `temper.toml` layer composition + the roster parse; binding is the spread in `expect` (40-composition, 'Binding is implicit'), carried as declaration rows, not composed from a manifest.
The roster/selection/admissibility over `temper.toml`'s `[role.*]` retires; whatever survives (requirement coverage over declaration rows) moves to the lock-rows source.

tests.asserts: reconciled or retired — no manifest-as-corpus remains
reconciled or retired — the manifest is no longer an authoring or gate surface

acceptance: No `temper.toml`/`roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` authoring or reading remains in the engine; the assembly is the SDK's `harness()` value compiled to lock declaration rows; contract/ byte-parity goldens reconciled to the lock/projection.
notes: Large retirement; the lib.rs `pub mod` line for the deleted assembly_artifacts module is dropped in this entry (linear chain — no co-edit conflict). contract/ byte-parity fixtures were the manifest/schema tripwire; reconcile them to the surviving projection/lock goldens.

summary: Retire KIND.md / PACKAGE.md parsing — a kind's schema is an SDK interface, a floor is an exported clause array; no kind/package file format

per.section: Decision: field typing lives in the SDK — there is no kind file format

edit.description: Retire the `KIND.md` header grammar (format/unit_shape/activation keys) and string-name identity resolution (15-kinds Decision: no kind file format; 'built-ins are a module, and identity is an import' rejects string-name identity + provider qualification). A kind's runtime residue is the five declaration facts read from the lock rows, not a parsed file.

The built-in floor no longer embeds/parses `packages/<name>/PACKAGE.md`; a floor is an exported clause array in `@temper/claude-code` (10-contracts) whose rows arrive via the seam, and the engine carries a compiled default program (50-distribution) for SDK-less checking.
The embedded built-in kind set becomes the compiled default program (declaration rows), not a walk over the `kinds/<provider>/<name>/KIND.md` tree.
Clause parsing off `PACKAGE.md` retires; clauses are compiled predicates arriving as declaration rows.
The KIND.md/PACKAGE.md document-carriage parse retires; the surface-language header grammar for kind/package files is gone.
The build-time embed no longer walks the `kinds/`/`packages/` `*.md` trees to embed KIND.md/PACKAGE.md; it embeds the compiled default program instead.

tests.asserts: reconciled — clauses come from compiled rows, not a parsed PACKAGE.md
reconciled or retired — no KIND.md/PACKAGE.md template parse remains
generic extraction driven by the five facts, no per-kind/file-format parse

acceptance: The engine parses no KIND.md or PACKAGE.md; kinds and floors are declaration rows (SDK-authored or the compiled default program); extraction is the generic algebra driven by the five facts.
notes: Terminal demolition of the file-format vehicle. Curated `kinds/`/`packages/` trees are fence-excluded human territory — the SESSION migrates/retires them after emit lands (inbox: fence-side moves are the session's hands). This entry retires only the engine-side parse.

summary: Collapse the four read traversals into one read verb — temper explain <target>

edit.description: Add `Command::Explain { target }` — one read verb over the graph `check` computes, answering member / requirement / leaf-grain address / neighborhood from a single `<target>` (20-surface Decision: one read verb, rejecting a verb per question). Projections, never gates; exits zero; discloses coverage (names what it cannot see).
Re-home the four traversals (why/requirements/impact/context) under `explain`, dispatched by the resolved target species. Reuse the graph-walk helpers left in place by CLI-COLLAPSE.
Reframe as explain tests: each target species walks its corner of the one graph; coverage disclosure asserted.

tests.asserts: explain <target> resolves member/requirement/address/neighborhood; never gates; discloses coverage

acceptance: `temper explain <target>` answers all four read questions over data check computes, exits zero, discloses coverage; the four separate verbs are gone.
notes: FORK-GATED on (explain-target-disambiguation): the corpus does not specify how one positional <target> resolves across member/requirement/address/neighborhood — member-vs-requirement name collision is the crux. Terminal leaf; blocks nothing. Human must rule target-resolution before build picks this.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm packages, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org + @temper scope, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end; channel 1 (SDK publish) also rides the SDK-primary foundation

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. Scoped `@temper` names per the resolved `(project-name)` fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring-face manifest — channel 1's `temper` core + `@temper/claude-code` provider module).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`temper` + `@temper/claude-code`, floors as exported clause arrays); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human creds.
